_provenance: Per-cohort hazard / odds ratios for KIM-1 as a prognostic biomarker for diabetic kidney disease progression. Cohort effect estimates quoted verbatim from Coca 2017 JASN (ACCORD + VA NEPHRON-D, PMC5576932) and Gutiérrez 2022 AJKD (CRIC, PMC9072594). All estimates are on the per-doubling-KIM-1 scale (i.e., per log2-unit). Coca 2017 reports ORs from a case-control / nested design and a HR from a prospective cohort; the analysis combines them on the log-effect scale per the published convention.

source_studies.citation: Coca SG, Nadkarni GN, Huang Y, et al. Plasma Biomarkers and Kidney Function Decline in Early and Established Diabetic Kidney Disease. JASN 2017;28(9):2786-2793.

Gutiérrez OM, Shlipak MG, Katz R, et al. Associations of Plasma Biomarkers of Inflammation, Fibrosis, and Kidney Tubular Injury With Progression of Diabetic Kidney Disease: A Cohort Study. AJKD 2022;79(6):849-857.

outcome: Composite of sustained eGFR decline and/or kidney failure (kidney function decline endpoint)
effect_metric: log-effect per doubling of KIM-1 (Model 3 / fully adjusted)
finerenone_relevance: These three cohorts establish the prognostic importance of KIM-1 in DKD progression. Finerenone is approved for DKD (FIDELIO-DKD, FIGARO-DKD) and its mechanism is plausibly modulated by tubular injury markers — these prognostic biomarkers contextualize trial-arm event rates.

trials.covariates: Model 3: treatment arm, baseline eGFR, ACR, age, race, SBP, DBP, BP/glucose/lipid meds
Model 3: treatment arm, baseline eGFR, ACR, age, race, SBP, DBP, BP/glucose/lipid meds
Model 3 (Table 3): demographics + clinical risk factors + baseline eGFR + UACR

_known_data_quality_notes: ACCORD result is OR (nested case-control) while NEPHRON-D and CRIC are HR (prospective cohort). The engine pools all three on the log-effect scale, treating log(OR) as numerically comparable to log(HR) for rare events; this is the standard approach in prognostic-factor MAs (Stijnen 2010 Stat Med). Document this in the methods narrative.
